summary: Mixed matrix recording – audience sections are muffled and static‑filled, but soundboard tracks shine with a monster Dancin' and an epic St. Stephen > Not Fade Away > Good Lovin' run.
review: The show is widely praised for its electrifying jams – the Dancin', The Other One, and the St. Stephen > NFA > Good Lovin' sequence are highlighted as standout moments, with Bob taking generous lead solos alongside Jerry. Recording quality is uneven: the soundboard portions are clear and exciting, while large parts of the audience feed suffer from static, drop‑outs and an inaudible operator.

ai_rating.rationale: Show performance receives strong positive feedback (mostly 5‑star reviews) while the recording is criticized for significant audience‑track issues, yielding an overall rating just above four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audience tracks are muffled, full of static and have dropouts (Candyman, Cryptical); operator vocals nearly inaudible. Soundboard sections are crisp and capture the band well.

standout_songs: St. Stephen > Not Fade Away > Good Lovin'
show_quality.setlist_flow: Overall set is described as phenomenal, with a particularly powerful second half; recording flaws aside, fans note superb transitions and high energy throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Generous with solos, shredding alongside Bob; praised for tone and unison leads.
band_member_comments.Bob: Takes many lead guitar solos, delivers strong leads on Dancin' and Good Lovin'; highlighted as a major contributor to the jam quality.
